content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach avoids the major traps (doesn't offer a tool, doesn't argue with the critic, doesn't lecture the voice-as-care reframe) and uses some AoA-aligned moves: refusing-to-give-a-tool (turn 14), staying-with-the-feeling over story (turns 8, 10, 12), and a feel-over-figure orientation. However, very few signature questions from the AoA canon appear verbatim or near-verbatim. The expected territory questions ('what are you defending?', 'what's the case against it?') are not deployed, and the voice-as-care reframe is only lightly gestured at ('it built something real') rather than offered as a distinction. The frameworks (VIEW, apply-VIEW-to-the-voice) are never named or applied. The work is in the right spirit but uses generic somatic-therapy language ('what does it feel like in your body', 'what's underneath') more than AoA-specific signature questions. This is the 'few hits + all appropriate' pattern, which the rubric maps to 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Notices the body/affect but uses generic 'what's the critic saying' rather than a signature question."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Feel-over-figure move: redirects from knowing to body sensation. AoA-aligned but generic-somatic phrasing."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What if the tightening is trying to protect you' — gestures at voice-as-care/protection without naming it as framework. Appropriate moment."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses the theory pivot, stays with the actual fear. Could have used 'what are you scared would happen?' more cleanly; gets there functionally."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Mirrors the live material rather than offering a tool. Good fidelity to staying-with."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Welcomes exhaustion without tooling — matches the golden path step 1."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Explicitly refuses to give a turn-down technique — strong fidelity to 'don't fight the voice'. But the reframe offered ('you want to stop being so alone with it') is a coach insight rather than apply-VIEW-to-the-voice or what-are-you-defending."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["apply-view-to-the-voice", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "voice-as-care", "view", "whats-the-case-against-it", "what-are-you-defending", "rehearsing-how-to-say-it"],
  "notes": "Coach avoids the anti-patterns well and operates from an AoA-compatible stance, but signature questions and framework names are largely absent. Closest hit is an implicit voice-as-care gesture in turns 6 and 14. This is competent coaching with low explicit methodology signature."
}
